macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Global Stock Markets Plunge in Selloff Triggered by Iran-US-Israel Conflict
items.summary: Major indices opened sharply lower across Asia and Europe, with Hang Seng down 1.22%, Nikkei down 1.49%, and STOXX Europe 600 down 1.76%, amid fears of oil supply disruptions from Strait of Hormuz tensions. US futures also tumbled, S&P 500 futures down 1.22% to 1.66%, Nasdaq 100 futures down 2.08%.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge on Fears of Strait of Hormuz Closure Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Higher oil and gas prices are expected due to potential disruption of 15% of global oil supply and 20% of LNG if the Strait of Hormuz closes, with forecasts of oil exceeding $100 per barrel. Defense stocks rallied, including BAE Systems up over 6% and Rheinmetall up 2%.

items.title: US Stock Futures Extend Losses with Nasdaq 100 Down 2.08%, Dow Down 1.64%
items.summary: Futures on major US indices weakened further, reflecting broad market pressure from ongoing global selloff linked to Middle East conflicts. No trading yet in US, Europe major indices closed or pre-open.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips to $63,000 Amid Middle East Tensions but Rebounds 5% to $66,500
items.summary: Escalating geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East, including Israeli and U.S. strikes on Iran, caused Bitcoin to drop to $63,000 before recovering nearly 2-5% despite equity market weakness and oil price surges. On-chain data shows contained impact with $1.8 billion in liquidations but no mass selling from short-term holders.

items.title: Uniswap Launches Multi-Chain Fee-Sharing Governance Vote for UNI Holders
items.summary: Uniswap initiated a proposal to redirect at least one-sixth of transaction fees from certain networks into a 'token jar' for UNI holders who burn equivalent UNI tokens. This aims to enhance token utility amid hot token activity.

items.title: Aave 'Will Win' Proposal Passes, Shifting to Fully Token-Centric Revenue Model
items.summary: The Aave governance vote to allocate 100% of product revenue directly to AAVE token holders has passed, transitioning Aave Labs toward a token-centric structure. AAVE emerges as a hot token alongside UNI and gold-backed assets.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin Outlines Ethereum Scalability Roadmap with Multidimensional Gas
items.summary: Ethereum's roadmap proceeds in short-term and long-term phases, introducing multidimensional gas to price resources independently and combat state bloat, with future zkEVM and Data Availability Sampling for 1,000x throughput boost.

items.title: Major Institutions Expand Crypto Services: SoFi Adds Solana, Morgan Stanley Seeks Trust License
items.summary: U.S. crypto bank SoFi now supports Solana deposits; Morgan Stanley applied for a trust bank license for Bitcoin custody, trading, yield, and lending. Additional moves include Citi's 2026 Bitcoin custody launch and SBI's yen-backed JPYSC stablecoin.

items.title: 40x Bitcoin Whale Liquidated 24 Times, Losing 94% of $2.66M After $66M Bet
items.summary: A highly leveraged Bitcoin whale faced 24 liquidations in 24 hours, with its balance crashing 94% following a massive $66M BTC position amid market volatility.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Ericsson Unveils AI-Ready Radio Portfolio at MWC 2026
items.summary: Ericsson announced ten new AI-ready radios with integrated neural network accelerators, five advanced antennas, and AI-powered RAN software features including AI-managed beamforming and latency optimization, positioning itself as an alternative to Nvidia-dependent solutions.

items.title: VirPoint Launches Hybrid AI Division for Financial Markets
items.summary: VirPoint announced a new AI Division featuring 'Hybrid Intelligence' that combines advanced AI tools with human financial expertise, including predictive stock ranking and hands-off portfolio management capabilities for institutional investors.

items.title: OpenAI Reaches Agreement with U.S. Department of War for AI Deployment
items.summary: OpenAI announced a classified deployment contract with the Department of War to deploy advanced AI systems in national security environments, emphasizing multi-layered safeguards and human oversight.

items.title: Research Reveals Cancer AI Tools May Rely on 'Shortcut Learning'
items.summary: University of Warwick research published in Nature Biomedical Engineering warns that popular deep learning systems for cancer pathology may use visual shortcuts rather than genuine biological signals, raising reliability concerns for clinical use.

items.title: System 2 Reasoning Models Emerge as Next AI Frontier
items.summary: Advanced 'System 2' AI models that simulate human cognitive processes and perform deep reasoning are gaining adoption across legal, financial, scientific, and software engineering applications, with OpenAI leading early development.

items.title: Machine Learning Transforms Marketing ROI Measurement
items.summary: AI and machine learning are becoming essential for modern marketing, enabling real-time personalization, predictive analytics, and autonomous campaign optimization, with future developments including generative systems and blockchain-backed solutions.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: March 2026 Video Game Release Calendar Announced with Major Titles
items.summary: Key March releases include Legacy of Kain: Defiance Remastered and Scott Pilgrim EX on March 3, Pokémon Pokopia on March 5, WWE 2K26 on March 13, and Super Mario Bros Wonder: Nintendo Switch 2 Edition on March 26 across PS5, Xbox, Switch 2, and PC.

items.title: Turtle Beach Lists All Major March 2026 Game Releases
items.summary: Highlights include Starship Troopers: Ultimate Bug War on March 16, Crimson Desert and Death Stranding 2: On the Beach on March 19, alongside Fatal Frame II: Crimson Butterfly Remake and Monster Hunter Stories 3 on March 12-13.

items.title: Biggest VR Games and Updates for March 2026 Revealed
items.summary: Gamertag VR covers Bootstrap Island full release, Star Trek VR, Blade & Sorcery Nomad's Sentara update, new Hitman VR mission with Milla Jovovich, and Iron Guard Salvation arriving on PSVR2 March 5.

items.title: Upcoming Nintendo Switch 2 Games for March 2026
items.summary: Nintendo Life details Pokémon Pokopia on March 5, Fatal Frame II: Crimson Butterfly Remake on March 12, Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection on March 13, and Super Mario Bros Wonder Switch 2 Edition on March 26.

items.title: Five New Steam Games You Probably Missed (March 2, 2026)
items.summary: PC Gamer spotlights recent releases like New Heights (rock climbing sim, Feb 27), Fear Effect 2: Retro Helix (PS1 port, Feb 28), and Emberbane (metroidvania, Feb 25) now available on Steam.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Lenovo Unveils Foldable Gaming Handheld and Modular PC Concept at MWC 2026
items.summary: Lenovo announced a wild foldable gaming handheld and a Modular AI PC concept with dual displays, detachable keyboard, and hot-swappable ports at MWC 2026, though both remain concepts for now. Additional launches include the Yoga 9i 2-in-1 Aura Edition Gen 11 and other productivity devices.[1][3]

items.title: Honor Reveals Robot Phone with 200MP Gimbal Camera at MWC 2026
items.summary: Honor showcased its Robot Phone featuring a 200-megapixel primary camera with three-axis gimbal stabilization, Super Steady Video, and AI Object Tracking, set for launch later this year. The device was highlighted among early MWC announcements.[1]

items.title: Lenovo Announces New Consumer Laptops, Tablets, and Accessories at MWC
items.summary: Lenovo launched the Yoga Slim 7i Aura Edition (March 2026, €1149 start), Idea Tab Pro Gen 2 tablet (March 2026, €549), Yoga Creative Keyboard AngryMiao Edition (June 2026, €259), and Yoga Pen Gen 2 with improved precision. These target creators, gamers, and productivity users.[3]

items.title: PC Prices Surge Due to Memory Shortages, Delaying Enterprise Upgrades
items.summary: Analysts predict sustained high PC prices from memory chip shortages diverted to AI servers, ending $500 consumer PCs and slowing enterprise upgrades; memory now 35% of PC costs, doubling historically. New Intel Panther Lake and AMD Ryzen AI PC chips are incoming but pricier.[4]

items.title: MWC 2026 Kicks Off with Phones, Laptops, Tablets, and Gadgets
items.summary: MWC 2026 began March 2 with pre-event announcements including new phones like Xiaomi 17 Ultra, laptops, tablets, and robots, providing hands-on previews of cutting-edge devices.[1]
